test.name: Report record matches for a tree person using person_record_matches
test.description: User invokes tree-edit directly and asks what records FamilySearch has matched to Patrick Flynn (FamilySearch ID LZNY-BRF). Tests that tree-edit calls person_record_matches and correctly reports the accepted attachment, the two pending hints, and their confidence levels — without making any edits to the tree.

input.user_message: tree-edit: Check what records are attached to this person — Patrick Flynn, FamilySearch ID LZNY-BRF. What hints does FamilySearch have for this person? Tell me which matches are accepted and which are pending.

judge_context: Should call person_record_matches with id LZNY-BRF (not a made-up ID)
Should clearly distinguish the accepted match (1850 census, confidence 5) from the two pending hints (1860 census confidence 3, Pennsylvania Death Certificates confidence 2)
Should NOT make any edits to tree.gedcomx.json or research.json — this is a read-only query, not an edit request
Should recommend the user review the two pending hints, especially the higher-confidence 1860 census match
